title: Lamb Tagine
description: A traditional Moroccan dish, lamb tagine is a hearty and flavorful stew made with tender lamb, gentle spices, and a sweet American malt.
instructions: Heat oil in the bottom of a clay or ceramic tagine over medium heat.
Add the onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic and ginger and cook for another minute.
Add the lamb and cook until browned on all sides.
Add the ras el hanout, malt syrup, broth, and bring to a simmer.
Cover the tagine and transfer to the oven.
Bake at 300°F (150°C) for 2-3 hours, or until the lamb is tender.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve hot over couscous or rice.
